resources.title: My microphone isn't working in Microsoft Teams
steps: For the best performance, make sure you have the latest version of Teams and the most recent Windows update or Mac OS update installed.
Make sure you're not muted.
In your meeting controls, make sure Mic is in an unmuted state.
If the mic icon has a line through it, you're currently on mute. Select Unmute mic to unmute. ​​​​​​​.
Check that your headphone boom is not in the mute position (usually upright or away from your mouth), or that your external mic's physical mute button is not turned on.

resources.title: Outlook shared mailbox missing or cannot expand the folder
steps: Open Outlook on the web first and check whether the shared mailbox is visible there. If it is, the problem is the desktop app profile, not mailbox access.
In Outlook desktop, go to File > Account Settings > Account Settings > your account > More Settings > Advanced and confirm the shared mailbox is still listed under additional mailboxes.
If it is missing or throws Cannot expand the folder, remove the shared mailbox entry, restart Outlook, and add the mailbox back so Outlook rebuilds that connection cleanly.
If the mailbox is gone in both web and desktop, ask the owner or admin to confirm your permission did not get removed.

resources.title: Outlook keeps suggesting an old email address in autocomplete
steps: Start typing the address in a new Outlook message, then use the arrow keys to highlight the wrong suggestion and press Delete so it is removed from the autocomplete cache.
If it still comes back, open the contact card and make sure the old address is removed from every email field, not just the main one.
Send one message to the correct address after clearing the old suggestion so Outlook learns the updated address first.
If the wrong address only appears on one device, clear it on that specific Outlook client because autocomplete caches are local.

resources.title: Teams meeting add-in missing from Outlook
steps: Close both Outlook and Teams fully, then reopen Teams first and Outlook second because the add-in often reloads in that order.
In Outlook, go to File > Options > Add-ins and confirm Microsoft Teams Meeting Add-in for Microsoft Office is listed under active or inactive COM add-ins rather than disabled add-ins.
If it is disabled, go to Manage: Disabled Items or COM Add-ins, re-enable the Teams add-in, and restart Outlook.
If the button is still missing, sign out and back in to Teams so the add-in can register again with Outlook.

resources.title: Word mangles spacing or bullets when pasting from the web
steps: Use Paste Special > Keep Text Only first so Word drops the web page formatting and keeps only the text.
If you need the bullets to stay, paste once as text only, then reapply Word bullets from the Home tab instead of keeping the browser formatting.
Go to File > Options > Advanced > Cut, copy, and paste and change Pasting from other programs to Keep Text Only if this happens often.
If only one site causes the mess, paste into Notepad first and then into Word to strip hidden web formatting completely.

resources.title: Excel formula bar or sheet tabs missing
steps: Go to the View tab in Excel and make sure Formula Bar is checked so the bar returns to the top of the workbook.
Open File > Options > Advanced and under Display options for this workbook, confirm Show sheet tabs is turned on.
If the workbook window is maximized oddly, drag the bottom or side edges back into place because hidden sheet tabs can be caused by the scroll bar taking the full space.
If only one workbook behaves this way, close it without saving layout changes, reopen it, and check whether the hidden elements return.

resources.title: SharePoint file locked or checked out when no one is editing it
steps: Open the file in SharePoint on the web and check whether it shows as Checked out or Locked in the file details pane.
If it says the file is checked out to you, use the file menu in SharePoint to check it back in or discard the checkout so the lock clears.
If the file is locked by your name from another device, close the file everywhere, wait a minute, and then reopen it from the browser instead of the synced desktop copy.
If the lock stays even after that, pause OneDrive sync briefly and retry from SharePoint on the web so the stale coauthoring session can expire.

resources.title: PowerPoint fonts changed on another laptop and layout shifted
steps: The other laptop is likely missing the original fonts, so open the deck on the affected device and note which fonts PowerPoint substituted.
If you own the source file, go to File > Options > Save and turn on Embed fonts in the file, then save and reopen the presentation on the other laptop.
If you cannot embed fonts, replace the missing font in PowerPoint with a standard font like Calibri or Aptos and then check the slide layout again.
For a live presentation, export a PDF backup so the layout stays stable even if the fonts are not installed.

resources.title: Excel keeps warning about external links after links were removed
steps: Go to Data > Workbook Links or Edit Links and confirm there are no remaining live links listed there.
If the warning still appears, check Name Manager because old named ranges can still point to external files even after formulas were changed.
Also check charts, conditional formatting rules, and data validation lists because hidden external references often live there.
If you need a quick cleanup, save a copy of the workbook and search for '[' in formulas and names because Excel stores external links with square brackets around the source workbook.

resources.title: Teams breakout rooms button missing for the organizer
steps: Confirm you joined the meeting as the organizer from the Teams desktop app because breakout room controls are limited or missing in some web and mobile situations.
Make sure the meeting is not using a simplified mode like Meet now in the wrong account context, because breakout rooms follow the organizer identity that scheduled the meeting.
Close and reopen Teams, then rejoin the meeting before checking for the Breakout rooms icon again.
If others can see the option but the organizer cannot, sign out of Teams and back in with the same account that created the meeting so the organizer role reloads correctly.

resources.title: Windows taskbar icons blank and Start search will not type
steps: Press Ctrl + Shift + Esc, open Task Manager, and restart Windows Explorer because blank taskbar icons and frozen search often clear after Explorer reloads.
If search still will not type, in Task Manager restart SearchHost.exe or SearchApp.exe if it appears in the process list.
Sign out of Windows and back in once to rebuild the taskbar and search session if Explorer restart only fixes part of the issue.
If this started right after an update, check Windows Update history for the exact update and note it before uninstalling anything.

resources.title: Outlook categories or search folders disappeared
steps: Check whether the mailbox is opening in Cached Exchange Mode and whether the same categories or search folders appear in Outlook on the web, because that tells you whether the loss is local or account-wide.
For missing search folders, right-click Search Folders and recreate the missing folder because those views can disappear if the local profile is damaged.
For missing categories, apply a category to one message and then open All Categories to confirm the master category list is still present.
If both categories and search folders vanished together, create a new Outlook profile or repair the mailbox profile because the local navigation pane may be corrupted.

resources.title: OneDrive Personal Vault asks for setup again after sign-in
keywords: asking me to set it up again
steps: Confirm you are signed in to the same Microsoft account that originally owned the Personal Vault, because switching accounts makes OneDrive offer setup again.
Open the OneDrive web view and check whether Personal Vault appears there first, because if it does the vault still exists and the desktop client just needs to refresh its account state.
Sign out of OneDrive from the desktop client, sign back in with the same account, and then open Personal Vault again so the client re-registers the vault state.
If it still asks to set it up from scratch, do not recreate it yet. Check the web view first so you do not confuse the existing vault with a fresh setup.

resources.title: OneDrive says shortcut target moved or desktop backup keeps re-enabling
steps: If OneDrive says a shortcut target moved, remove the broken shortcut from OneDrive on the web first and then recreate it from the current folder or library location.
For desktop backup re-enabling itself, open OneDrive Settings > Sync and backup > Manage backup and confirm which folders are actually selected there.
Turn the backup off from Manage backup rather than just moving folders around in File Explorer, because OneDrive will keep reapplying the managed backup state otherwise.
After changing the backup setting, restart OneDrive once so the client reloads the current folder mapping.

resources.title: Microsoft account says too many requests or Authenticator approval is not arriving
steps: Stop retrying for a few minutes first, because repeated sign-in attempts can trigger a temporary Microsoft security throttle.
After waiting, try one fresh sign-in attempt and choose a different verification method if one is available instead of repeatedly resending Authenticator approval.
Check the Authenticator app on the phone directly and make sure the account tile is still present and the phone time is set automatically.
If approval still does not arrive after the cooldown, use the Microsoft account recovery path or open a ticket with the exact sign-in message you saw.

resources.title: Teams says you are muted or camera is black in one meeting only
steps: Leave and rejoin that one meeting first, because a single meeting can keep a stale device session even when Teams is fine everywhere else.
In Teams meeting controls, open Device settings and reselect the microphone and camera manually instead of leaving them on the previous remembered device.
If the camera preview stays black only in that meeting, stop video and start it again after switching devices once.
If the mic problem and black camera both happen only in one meeting, ask the organizer whether another app or browser has captured the same devices through the meeting link.

resources.title: PowerPoint embedded video shows black in presenter view
steps: Test the deck outside slide show first and confirm the video plays on the slide itself before entering Presenter View.
In Slide Show settings, turn Presenter View off once and test again because the secondary-display handoff can blank media on the presenter screen.
If the video file was linked rather than embedded, copy the media file to the same laptop and relink it or embed it again before presenting.
For the live session, keep a backup copy of the video outside PowerPoint so you can play it separately if Presenter View blanks it again.

resources.title: Word markup still appears in print preview after Accept All
steps: Go to Review and confirm Track Changes is turned off first, because Accept All does not stop new markup if tracking is still enabled.
Open the display dropdown under Review and switch from All Markup to No Markup, then check Print Preview again.
In File > Print, check whether Print Markup is still turned on, because that setting can keep comments and revisions in the print view even after changes were accepted.
If the document still shows old revision balloons, save a copy, reopen it, and check whether comments or unresolved formatting marks are still present in the copy.

resources.title: Outlook shared or delegate calendar is not updating
steps: Open the same calendar in Outlook on the web first so you can tell whether the stale view is only in desktop Outlook or in the mailbox itself.
In Outlook desktop, switch away from the calendar and back, then uncheck and recheck the shared calendar so Outlook reloads that cached view.
If the web calendar is current but desktop is stale, remove the shared calendar from Outlook and add it again so the local cache rebuilds cleanly.
If both web and desktop show old data, ask the calendar owner or admin to confirm your delegate permission and sync path did not change.

resources.title: Outlook shared mailbox opens blank after access changed
steps: Check the shared mailbox in Outlook on the web first, because if it loads there the issue is the desktop cache and not the mailbox permission itself.
If Outlook desktop still shows the mailbox but opens it blank, remove the shared mailbox from the account's Advanced settings, restart Outlook, and add it back.
If the mailbox is blank in both web and desktop, ask the owner or admin to confirm your access was not reduced to a different permission level.
After the permission is confirmed, reopen Outlook fresh so the desktop profile pulls the current mailbox rights instead of the old cached state.

resources.title: Excel formulas refer to invalid names or Name Manager is broken
steps: Open Formulas > Name Manager and look for names with #REF! or names pointing to old sheets or deleted ranges.
Delete or repair the broken names first, because Excel can keep warning even after the visible formulas look correct.
Check conditional formatting, charts, and data validation too, because named ranges can stay hidden in those places.
Save a copy of the workbook after repairing the names, close Excel, and reopen the copy to confirm the warning is gone.

resources.title: Excel keeps asking to update links from a retired workbook
steps: Go to Data > Workbook Links or Edit Links and confirm which retired workbook Excel still thinks is attached.
If no live formulas point there anymore, check Name Manager, charts, and conditional formatting because old links often hide in those places.
Search the workbook for '[' because Excel stores external workbook links with square brackets around the source file name.
After removing the last hidden link, save a copy of the workbook and reopen it to confirm the update-links prompt is gone.

resources.title: Teams join button is greyed out or disabled for a meeting
steps: Open the same meeting from Teams Calendar and Outlook and confirm the Join button is disabled in both places, because that helps separate a bad local client state from a meeting-policy issue.
Sign out of Teams fully, reopen it, and check the meeting again because a stale meeting cache can leave one invite stuck in the wrong state.
If the meeting opens fine in Teams on the web but not in the desktop app, stay with the web join for now and clear the desktop Teams cache after the meeting.
If only this one invite is affected, ask the organizer to resend or update the meeting because the join metadata in the original invite may be broken.

resources.title: Outlook resolves a coworker to the wrong directory person
keywords: wrong dan in our company directory
steps: Start a new message and remove the cached name suggestion first so Outlook does not keep preferring the stale resolved entry.
Use Check Names or the address book picker and confirm you are selecting the correct directory entry with the right department or email address.
If Outlook keeps choosing the wrong internal person automatically, remove any local contact entry that has the same display name because local contacts can override directory resolution.
If the wrong person still resolves everywhere, ask your admin to check the display names in the company directory because the issue may be upstream from Outlook.

resources.title: PowerPoint presenter notes are tiny or scaled wrong on the presenter monitor
keywords: notes are huge on the presenter screen
steps: Open Slide Show > Set Up Slide Show and confirm Presenter View is using the intended secondary display instead of the wrong monitor mapping.
In Windows Display Settings, make sure the presenter screen is set as the secondary display with the expected scaling before reopening PowerPoint.
If the notes pane is still unreadable, turn Presenter View off once, start the slide show, stop it, then turn Presenter View back on so PowerPoint rebuilds the monitor layout.
For a live session, keep a printed or separate notes copy handy if the lectern screen keeps scaling unpredictably.

resources.title: SharePoint properties or required columns panel is stuck loading
keywords: required columns panel is stuck loading
steps: Open the library in a full browser tab and try the file details pane there first, because the embedded panel can hang while the main site still works.
Refresh the library and test the properties panel in an InPrivate or another browser session so you can rule out a stuck cached script.
If the required columns still will not load, open the library settings and confirm the columns still exist and were not recently changed in a way that broke the form.
If only one file is affected, try opening its details from the file list instead of Office desktop so SharePoint can load the metadata form directly.
